notes_history.author: Hollis Akins
notes_history.note_text: DSFG? Appears to have strong [NII] in grating

notes_history.author: Hollis Akins
notes_history.note_text:
LRD, similar redshift as ember_uds_p1_24588, but this one has no Lya. 
Broad Ha and Hb in the grating, maybe Hg+[OIII]4363 faintly detected as well.

notes_history.author: Hollis Akins
notes_history.note_text: A beautiful brown dwarf spectrum... oops! Was selected as a z~7 galaxy, common contaminant situation

notes_history.author: Hollis Akins
notes_history.note_text: Selected as a particularly bright LRD with a photo-z~7. Broad Ha and Hb in the grating, also Lya, maybe some faint UV lines?

notes_history.author: Hollis Akins
notes_history.note_text: Crazy strong broad line, Balmer break, FeII+MgII

notes_history.author: Hollis B Akins
notes_history.note_text: Nothing in the G395M spectrum but should be given the PRISM; probably stuck closed shutter in G395M mask?

notes_history.author: Hollis B Akins
notes_history.note_text: OIII doublet at ~5.1µm is probably contamination from overlapping spectra, line at ~3.25µm looks real though. Needs manual review in combination with photoz.

notes_history.author: Hollis Akins
notes_history.note_text: Contamination from nearby LRD 10059, what looks like an OIII doublet is in fact the OIII doublet from 10059, barely making it through the F290LP filter.

notes_history.author: Hollis Akins
notes_history.note_text: Selected as LRD, no clear broad Hbeta, but booming Halpha just at the edge of the detector; probably broadended because otherwise we wouldn't see it. Could be worth extending the wavelength range in a future reduction.

notes_history.author: Hollis Akins
notes_history.note_text: Selected as LRD, unclear if it has any broad lines though
